tier1.title: Conceptual Foundation
tier1.content: Rotational motion parallels linear motion with angular quantities. What object is rotating, and about which axis? What causes the rotation — a force applied at a distance from the axis?

tier2.title: Relevant Principles
tier2.content: Torque: τ = rF sin θ = Iα. Rotational kinematics: ω = ω₀ + αt, θ = ω₀t + ½αt². Moment of inertia I depends on mass distribution (I = Σmr²). Rotational KE: KE_rot = ½Iω². Angular momentum L = Iω, conserved if τ_net = 0.

tier3.title: Targeted Guidance
tier3.content: For torque, use the lever arm — the perpendicular distance from axis to the line of action of the force. A force through the axis produces zero torque. Draw a diagram showing r and θ. For rolling: v = rω and consider both translation and rotation.

tier4.title: Near-Solution Scaffold
tier4.content: Calculate τ = rF sin θ for each force, with sign for direction (CW vs CCW). Write τ_net = Iα. Look up or compute I for the shape (disk: ½MR², rod about end: ⅓ML², etc.). For rolling without slipping: use energy ½mv² + ½Iω² = mgh or force/torque analysis.

tier5.title: Full Solution Placeholder
tier5.content: Tier 5 is generated by the AI tutor with a complete solution and reasoning comparison.

tier1.title: Conceptual Foundation
tier1.content: Every point on a rigid rotating body has the same angular velocity ω, but different linear speeds v = rω. Is the object in pure rotation or rolling? What is the pivot or axis?

tier2.title: Relevant Principles
tier2.content: Rolling without slipping: v_cm = Rω, a_cm = Rα. Total KE for rolling: KE = ½mv_cm² + ½Iω². Parallel axis theorem: I = I_cm + Md². Static equilibrium: ΣF = 0 AND Στ = 0.

tier3.title: Targeted Guidance
tier3.content: For equilibrium problems, choose a pivot to eliminate unknown forces — torques about that point simplify. For rolling down incline, friction provides torque but does no work in rolling without slipping (contact point instantaneously at rest).

tier4.title: Near-Solution Scaffold
tier4.content: Write Newton's 2nd law for translation: mg sin θ − f = ma. Torque about CM: fR = Iα. Use a = Rα and eliminate f. Or use energy: mgh = ½mv² + ½I(v/R)². Solve for acceleration or speed at bottom.

tier5.title: Full Solution Placeholder
tier5.content: Tier 5 is generated by the AI tutor with a complete solution and reasoning comparison.
